profile: Licensed journeyman plumber with 6 years of experience in residential and commercial plumbing installation, maintenance, and repair. Completed a 4-year apprenticeship through the United Association and holds a Washington State journeyman plumber license. Led crews on new construction projects and managed service calls independently. Known for clean work, code compliance, and reliable communication with customers and general contractors.

certificates.name: Washington State Journeyman Plumber License
certificates.issuer: Washington State Department of Labor and Industries

skills.text: PEX, copper, CPVC, cast iron, ABS

bullet_points.text: Handle new construction rough-in and trim-out on residential and light commercial projects, averaging 3–4 active jobs per month
Lead service calls independently, diagnosing and resolving issues including slab leaks, drain blockages, water heater failures, and fixture replacements
Mentored 2 apprentices over the past year, supervising their work and signing off on apprenticeship hour logs
Maintain a 4.9-star customer rating across 60+ reviewed service calls tracked by company dispatch software
Led a 3-person rough-in crew on a 48-unit mixed-use building, completing all water supply, DWV, and gas rough-in on schedule across 6 floors with zero correction notices on 4 inspections
Installed complete commercial kitchen plumbing on a restaurant tenant improvement, working within a 3-week phased shutdown window to minimize closure time
Completed a full copper-to-PEX repipe on a 2,400 sq ft 1960s home solo, restoring full service within 2 days as promised and identifying two additional failing components during the job
Managed material ordering and staging for crew on multi-week projects, reducing downtime from shortages to near zero
Completed 8,000-hour UA apprenticeship across residential, commercial, and industrial job sites under journeyman and foreman supervision
Progressed from helper tasks to independent rough-in work by year 3; completed final apprenticeship year working unsupervised on service calls
Passed Washington State journeyman plumber licensing exam on first attempt with a score of 89%
Assisted customers with product selection in the plumbing department and completed the Home Depot plumbing product certification program
Managed department inventory and handled special orders for contractors
